preamble: The diphthong /uo/, particularly when the initial /u/ is unstressed or occurs in specific phonetic contexts, can undergo consonantalization. This process transforms the vocalic element of the diphthong into the voiced labio-velar approximant /w/, effectively reducing the diphthong and strengthening its high vowel component into a consonantal glide.

examples.note: The suffix *-uo- is attested to have become -w- in Oscan.
The initial element of the diphthong consonantalizes to /w/.
The initial element of the diphthong consonantalizes to /w/.

sources: Buck, C. D. (1904). A Grammar of Oscan and Umbrian: With a Collection of Inscriptions and a Glossary. Ginn & Company.
Visser, W. (1997). The Syllable in Frisian. Holland Institute of Generative Linguistics.
